name: Port Arthur Teachers
description_short: Port Arthur Teachers Federal Credit Union offers banking, lending, and ATM services to residents, employees, and families within the Port Arthur Independent School District.
description_long: Port Arthur Teachers Federal Credit Union (PATFCU) has evolved from its origins serving educators to become a community financial institution. Originally established to serve teachers, the credit union has expanded eligibility to anyone living, working, worshiping, or attending school within Port Arthur Independent School District boundaries, plus immediate family members of current members. This democratization of membership reflects a shift toward broader community service while maintaining roots in the education sector. PATFCU offers a comprehensive suite of banking and lending products. Members can access checking and savings accounts, online and mobile banking through their enrollment portal, auto loans (as low as 4.50% APR), personal loans (as low as 9.25% APR), annual loans (as low as 7.00% APR), and Mastercard credit products (9.90% APR). The credit union provides surcharge-free ATM access through the Allpoint Network and Transfund Network, expanding convenience beyond their physical locations. They also administer an annual scholarship program for community members. PATFCU distinguishes itself through competitive loan rates, community-focused membership eligibility, and integration into established ATM networks for broader accessibility. The credit union maintains a local presence with a physical branch while offering digital banking options. Their scholarship program demonstrates commitment to educational advancement within the community they serve. As a traditional credit union, PATFCU functions as a straightforward banking option rather than a specialized financial service provider. Members should understand that credit union membership requires eligibility verification, and while loan rates are competitive, approval depends on creditworthiness. The institution provides standard banking services without specialized offerings in debt relief, credit repair, or alternative lending.
services: Surcharge-free ATM access via Allpoint Network
Surcharge-free ATM access via Transfund Network
pros: Competitive auto loan rates starting at 4.50% APR
Low personal loan rates beginning at 9.25% APR
Surcharge-free ATM access through Allpoint and Transfund networks
Broad membership eligibility including community residents, not just teachers
Annual scholarship program supporting educational goals
Online account access and enrollment available
Automatic telephone system (PATTI) for account inquiries
cons: Membership restricted to Port Arthur Independent School District boundaries and their families
No specialized credit repair or debt relief services mentioned
Limited geographic footprint as a single-location credit union
Website lacks detail on checking/savings account features and fees
No mention of business banking or commercial lending services
best_for: Port Arthur area residents and workers seeking competitive loan rates
Teachers and education employees within the school district
Families of current credit union members looking for banking services
Community members prioritizing local financial institution membership
diagnosis: PATFCU is best for individuals living or working in Port Arthur, Texas who want straightforward banking services and competitive loan rates from a community-focused credit union. The primary caveat is membership eligibility—you must live, work, worship, or attend school within Port Arthur ISD boundaries or be an immediate family member of a current member.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
meta_description: Port Arthur Teachers Federal Credit Union offers banking, lending, and ATM services to ISD employees and families.
